LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT SAN FRANCISCO BAY CONSERVATION AND DEVELOPMENT'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE AND COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES AND DECLARATORY RELIEF - THE DEMURRER TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1-3 ARE SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. AT PARAGRAPH 32 OF THE COMPLAINT/PETITION, PLAINTIFF CONCEDES THAT THE DEMOLITION OF THE HARBOR IS COMPLETE. ACCORDINGLY, THOSE CLAIMS ARE MOOT AND PLAINTIFF FAILS TO HIGHLIGHT ANY GERMANE EXCEPTION TO THE MOOTNESS DOCTRINE. SEE WILSON & WILSON V. CITY COUNCIL OF REDWOOD CITY (2011) 191 CAL.APP.4TH 1559. THE DEMURRERS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 4 AND 5 ARE OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF CAN MAINTAIN THE CLAIMS BASED ON THE FEDERAL CONSTITUTION AND 42 USC 1983. IT IS PREMATURE TO DETERMINE WHETHER DEFENDANT IS ENTITLED TO 11TH AMENDMENT IMMUNITY AS AN ARM OF THE STATE. SEE LYNCH V. SFHA (1997) 55 CAL.APP.4TH 527. THE COURT DENIES DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE UNDER EVID. 452(C) AND THE ATTACHED DOCUMENT DOES NOT CLEARLY SHOW THAT DEFENDANT IS AN ARM OF THE STATE. THE COURT SUSTAINS THE DEMURRER TO CAUSE OF ACTION 6 WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. AN INJUNCTION IS NOT A DISTINCT CAUSE OF ACTION AND CAUSES OF ACTION 4 AND 5 SEEK INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. SEE SHELL OIL CO. V. MARINAS FOR ALL (1942) 52 CAL.APP.2D 164. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

LAW AND MOTION 302, REAL PARTY IN INTEREST PETE'S ENTERPRISES, INC.'S JOINDER IN THE DEMURRER OF SAN FRANCISCO BAY CONSERVATION AND DEVELOPMENT COMMISSION TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE AND COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES AND DECLARATORY RELIEF - THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION AS AGAINST THE REAL PARTY IN INTEREST. THE DEMURRER TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1-3 ARE SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. AT PARAGRAPH 32 OF THE COMPLAINT/PETITION, PLAINTIFF CONCEDES THAT THE DEMOLITION OF THE HARBOR IS COMPLETE. ACCORDINGLY, THOSE CLAIMS ARE MOOT AND PLAINTIFF FAILS TO HIGHLIGHT ANY GERMANE EXCEPTION TO THE MOOTNESS DOCTRINE. SEE WILSON & WILSON V. CITY COUNCIL OF REDWOOD CITY (2011) 191 CAL.APP.4TH 1559. PLAINTIFF CANNOT ALLEGE CAUSES OF ACTION 4 AND 5 AGAINST REAL PARTY IN INTEREST BECAUSE PLAINTIFF PLEADS NO FACTS SHOWING THAT REAL PARTY IS A STATE ACTOR. THE COURT SUSTAINS THE DEMURRER TO CAUSE OF ACTION 6 WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. AN INJUNCTION IS NOT A DISTINCT CAUSE OF ACTION AND CAUSES OF ACTION 4 AND 5 SEEK INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. SEE SHELL OIL CO. V. MARINAS FOR ALL (1942) 52 CAL.APP.2D 164. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, Int He Alternative, Aummary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-23-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT SAN FRANCISCO BAY CONSERVATION AND DEVELOPMENT COMMISSION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In the Alternative, Summary Adjudication is granted. No opposition filed. Respondent San Francisco Bay Conservation and Development Commission met its initial burden by showing that it is a state agency and is therefore immune from suit under 42 U.S.C. sec 1983. (Undisputed Material Facts 1-14.) PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/AJR)

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Feb-18-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. BRYAN HAMHIR, TEL. NO. (916) 447-0700 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. CHRISTIANA TIEDEMAN, TEL. NO. (510) 622-2218 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT;
ATTY. WILLIAM R. WARHUST OF HANNING LAW FIRM, LLP, TEL. NO. (650) 482-3040 APPEARING FOR THE REAL PARTY IN INTERES, PETE'S ENTERPRISES, INCORPORATED BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT SAN FRANCISCO BAY CONSERVATION AND DEVELOPMENT's DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE AND COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES AND DECLARATORY RELIEF - The demurrer to causes of action 1-3 are sustained without leave to amend. At paragraph 32 of the complaint/petition, plaintiff concedes that the demolition of the harbor is complete. Accordingly, those claims are moot and plaintiff fails to highlight any germane exception to the mootness doctrine. See Wilson & Wilson v. City Council of Redwood City (2011) 191 Cal.App.4th 1559. The demurrers to causes of action 4 and 5 are overruled. Plaintiff can maintain the claims based on the federal constitution and 42 USC 1983. It is premature to determine whether defendant is entitled to 11th Amendment immunity as an arm of the state. See Lynch v. SFHA (1997) 55 Cal.App.4th 527. The Court denies defendant's request for judicial notice under Evid. 452(c) and the attached document does not clearly show that defendant is an arm of the state. The Court sustains the demurrer to cause of action 6 without leave to amend. An injunction is not a distinct cause of action and causes of action 4 and 5 seek injunctive relief. See Shell Oil Co. v. Marinas for All (1942) 52 Cal.App.2d 164. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Feb-18-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. BRYAN HAMHIR, TEL. NO. (916) 447-0700 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. CHRISTIANA TIEDEMAN, TEL. NO. (510) 622-2218 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT;
ATTY. WILLIAM R. WARHUST OF HANNING LAW FIRM, LLP, TEL. NO. (650) 482-3040 APPEARING FOR THE REAL PARTY IN INTEREST, PETE'S ENTERPRISES, INCORPORATED BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. REAL PARTY IN INTEREST PETE'S ENTERPRISES, INC.'S JOINDER IN THE DEMURRER OF SAN FRANCISCO BAY CONSERVATION AND DEVELOPMENT COMMISSION TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE AND COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES AND DECLARATORY RELIEF - The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to all causes of action as against the real party in interest. The demurrer to causes of action 1-3 are sustained without leave to amend. At paragraph 32 of the complaint/petition, plaintiff concedes that the demolition of the harbor is complete. Accordingly, those claims are moot and plaintiff fails to highlight any germane exception to the mootness doctrine. See Wilson & Wilson v. City Council of Redwood City (2011) 191 Cal.App.4th 1559. Plaintiff cannot allege causes of action 4 and 5 against real party in interest because plaintiff pleads no facts showing that real party is a state actor. The Court sustains the demurrer to cause of action 6 without leave to amend. An injunction is not a distinct cause of action and causes of action 4 and 5 seek injunctive relief. See Shell Oil Co. v. Marinas for All (1942) 52 Cal.App.2d 164. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION

MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT SAN FRANCISCO BAY CONSERVATION AND DEVELOPMENT's DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is Continued to February 18, 2014 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than January 22, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.
